step.title: Step 43
step.actionDisplay: SPACE -> SPACE -> SPACE -> SPACE -> SPACE -> RESET

sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 5 actions in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: SPACE, SPACE, SPACE, SPACE, SPACE.
You are still on the same level.
The game is over.
Current state: step 138, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, SPACE, MOUSE, ACTION7.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `last_animation`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`.
`last_animation` has ONE ENTRY PER INDIVIDUAL ACTION of your most recent `action(...)` call, in execution order; each entry's `.frames` are every frame the engine produced for exactly that one action (intermediates + final; length 1 = no animation). After action(['UP','UP','LEFT']) there are three entries -- first UP, second UP, then LEFT. All change within one entry's frames is that single action animating, never several actions. Step through entries to read per-move motion and causality.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: Game over after 137 actions. Board reset - character now in LEFT panel, L-shapes in RIGHT panel. Need to understand new configuration and find efficient way to consume L-shapes.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 137 actions; a strong score needs about 32 or fewer. You are 4.3x over the target.
NET-ZERO WASTE: your last 68 actions returned the board to a state already seen 68 actions ago — that round-trip made no progress yet still cost 68 actions. Do not extend-and-retract or oscillate; a sequence that ends where it began is pure waste.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the current state after the reset:
- Character (id=4, black, 40px) at [[15, 18], [15, 26], [23, 26], [23, 24]] - rows 15-23, cols 18-26
- Charcoal L-shape (id=5, 45px) at [[15, 36], [15, 44], [17, 44], [17, 39]] - rows 15-17, cols 36-44
- Yellow L-shape (id=11, 45px) at [[45, 51], [45, 59], [47, 59], [47, 54]] - rows 45-47, cols 51-59
- Yellow border bar (id=3, 64px) at [[0, 63], [63, 63]] - full bar
- White eyes at (16, 19), (16, 22), (16, 25), (19, 25), (22, 25)
So the character is at rows 15-23, cols 18-26 (left-center of the board). The charcoal L-shape is at rows 15-17, cols 36-44 (right-center of the board). The yellow L-shape is at rows 45-47, cols 51-59 (bottom-right).
The character is in the left panel (cols 18-26), and the charcoal L-shape is in the right panel (cols 36-44). The light blue bar is at column 32.
Wait, but the character is at cols 18-26, which is to the left of the light blue bar (col 32). The charcoal L-shape is at cols 36-44, which is to the right of the light blue bar. So the character is in the left panel, and the charcoal L-shape is in the right panel.
But wait, looking at the initial state from the first turn, the character was at cols 51-59 (right panel), and the charcoal L-shape was at cols 3-11 (left panel). Now the character is at cols 18-26 (left panel), and the charcoal L-shape is at cols 36-44 (right panel). So the positions have swapped.
Actually, I think the light blue bar is at column 32, and the left panel is cols 0-31, right panel is cols 33-63. The character is at cols 18-26 (left panel), and the charcoal L-shape is at cols 36-44 (right panel).
But wait, the character is at cols 18-26, which is in the left panel. The charcoal L-shape is at cols 36-44, which is in the right panel. So the character can't reach the charcoal L-shape.
Hmm, but the character is at rows 15-23, and the charcoal L-shape is at rows 15-17. They're at the same row range. But they're in different panels.
Wait, I need to reconsider. The light blue bar is at column 32. The character is at cols 18-26, which is to the left of the bar. The charcoal L-shape is at cols 36-44, which is to the right of the bar. So the character is in the left panel, and the charcoal L-shape is in the right panel.
But the character can't cross the light blue bar. So the character can't reach the charcoal L-shape.
Wait, but in the previous run, the character was in the right panel and could move around. The L-shapes were in both panels. The character could consume the yellow L-shape in the right panel by overlapping with it.
In this new state, the character is in the left panel, and there's no L-shape in the left panel. So the character can't consume any L-shapes.
But wait, maybe the character can cross the light blue bar? Let me try moving RIGHT to see if the character can cross the bar.
Actually, looking at the current image, the character is at the top-left of the left panel. The charcoal L-shape is at the top-right of the right panel. The yellow L-shape is at the bottom-right of the right panel.
Let me try moving RIGHT to see if the character can cross the light blue bar and reach the charcoal L-shape.
Actually, I realize I need to understand the game better. Let me try a different approach. Let me move the character RIGHT towards the charcoal L-shape and see what happens.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content: World model: Game reset. Character at rows 15-23, cols 18-26 (left panel). Charcoal L-shape at rows 15-17, cols 36-44 (right panel). Yellow L-shape at rows 45-47, cols 51-59 (bottom-right). Yellow border bar full at 64px. Character and charcoal L-shape at same row range but different panels. Let me try moving RIGHT to cross the light blue bar and reach the charcoal L-shape.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Move RIGHT towards the charcoal L-shape to see if character can cross the light blue bar
action(['RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
